data.title: Microservice Design
data.description: Apple.com uses microservices architecture to handle various services like store, support, and media content.
data.technical_spec: Services are designed to be independent and scalable, using containerization and orchestration technologies.

data.title: Cloud Integration
data.description: Website backend is heavily integrated with cloud services for efficient load balancing and data distribution.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes cloud providers for CDN and redundant server systems to ensure high availability.

data.title: API-led Connectivity
data.description: APIs are extensively used to provide seamless interaction between different parts of the site and other external Apple services.
data.technical_spec: RESTful and GraphQL APIs employed for inter-service communication.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Uses multiple databases for managing user data, product information and transactional services.
data.technical_spec: Combination of SQL and NoSQL databases to optimize for read and write operations.

data.title: Responsive Design
data.description: Ensures compatibility and optimal usability across a wide range of devices and screen sizes.
data.technical_spec: Uses frameworks like Bootstrap to implement flexible grids and media queries.
